title: LM_TIMEOUT
text: LM_TIMEOUT The default timeout to connect to a Sentinel RMS server is 30 seconds. If the RMS server does not respond within that time, Uniface will stop trying and report an error. But 30 seconds may be too long or too short, so LM_TIMEOUT can be used to set a different timeout (in seconds). However, when LM_REDUNDANT is used, the timeout is set to just one second. The reason for this is that if the RMS server that was initially assigned the role of leader is down, then the login to a license feature will wait the full timeout period to get its license from the original leader, and only when that has expired try to get it from the actual leader. Since Sentinel allows minimum one second time, LM_REDUNDANT will use this time as the duration for network timeout. However, if you have a slow or busy network, one second may be too short. If one second is too short, then you may experience errors that you do not get if you do not use LM_REDUNDANT . You can increase LM_TIMEOUT to two or more seconds to solve this situation. An example for a pool of three redundant RMS servers on a slow network: $license_options LM_LICENSE_FILE=rms.serverA;rms.serverB;rms.serverC, LM_REDUNDANT, LM_TIMEOUT=2 Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
